Porcelain veneers ✦Clear aligners ✦BL1 whitening ✦Digital smile design ✦Composite edge bonding ✦

ØA point of view

Vanity is just attention to detail wearing better clothes. We engineer it in tenths of a millimetre — then make it look like you were simply born lucky.

Most cosmetic dentistry fails by being visible. Too white, too flat, too uniform — teeth that announce themselves. Our standard is the opposite: ceramics layered with the translucency of real enamel, movement no one clocks, brightness held one notch inside believable.

Every case is designed against your face — lip line, smile arc, the way you actually laugh — and finished by hand in our in-house lab.

0.3mm The thickness our ceramicists routinely work to — about three human hairs.

The reference is never a paint chip. It is the patient — their skin, their age, the light they live in. We design to that, and only that.

11The work

FOUR
DISCIPLINES.

One standard for all of them: it has to look like luck, and last like engineering.

V—01 · Porcelain veneers8–10 units · 3 visits

Porcelain veneers

Hand-layered feldspathic ceramic, 0.3 mm thin, with the depth and translucency of natural enamel. Shaped to your face — never to a template.

Enquire →
A—02 · Clear aligners6–18 months

Clear aligners

Orthodontics nobody clocks. Invisalign® and in-house systems, with movement mapped to 0.05 mm per stage and reviewed monthly.

Enquire →
W—03 · Whitening2 visits · up to 8 shades

The BL1 protocol

From A3 to bleach-shade without the chalk. Enamel-safe, sensitivity-managed, and stopped deliberately half a shade inside believable.

Enquire →
D—04 · Smile design1 visit · 3D preview

Digital smile design

Your future smile, drafted in 3D against a scan of your face — then worn out of the chair as a same-day trial. Edit it like a fitting.

Enquire →

12VITA-aligned shade map

We argue in half-shades.

Target ▸ BL1 — reached in 2 visits

Every case is mapped on a calibrated VITA scale before a single instrument is lifted. Brightness is a decision, not an accident.

A4 → BL1

21The protocol

FOUR
VISITS,
FLAWLESS.

A fixed sequence, because precision is a habit, not a mood. Most smile designs complete inside three weeks.

01

Scan

A full digital impression of your mouth and face. No goo, no gagging, no guesswork — just data.

Intraoral 3D
60 µm accuracy
02

Design

Your smile drafted against your features — lip line, golden proportion, the way you actually laugh. Reviewed with you, edge by edge.

Facially-driven
DSD workflow
03

Preview

A trial smile, worn home the same day. Live with it, photograph it, change your mind. Nothing is permanent yet.

Mock-up
Same-day fit
04

Place

Final ceramics bonded under calibrated curing light. The blue flash is the moment it becomes permanent — and the last time anyone should notice your dentistry.

450 nm cure
Rubber-dam isolation

31Recent work

WORN
DAILY.

Imagery is representative — patient privacy is non-negotiable. Full case files are shown privately, in the chair.

41The atelier

Led by Dr. Meera Nair, prosthodontist — with a master ceramicist on staff, which is rarer than it should be.

Fourteen years, four thousand units of ceramic, and a standing rule: if a stranger can tell it’s dentistry, it goes back to the lab. The atelier runs its own milling and layering room two doors from the chair, so nothing about your case is outsourced — not the design, not the shade, not the responsibility.

One patient at a time. Phones off. Coffee decent.

14yIn practice
4k+Ceramic units placed
In-houseLab & ceramicist
